threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube'; locals just say 'the underground' or 'the train' like they own it.
Saw a bloke on the 208 talking to a kebab while waiting for his stop, arguing about the toppings. Classic Bromley vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a cat lounging on the tram tracks at Karl-Marx-Strasse while everyone just walked by—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a late-night Döner spot that doubles as an art gallery. I swear, I once saw a performance art piece while waiting for my kebab.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here when they dodge the street performer with a half-eaten burrito, but still stop for a quick chat with the taco truck guy.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo on the 19 Polk, scrolling through wedding pics while the bus lurched. Only in San Francisco does your big day start with Muni chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are by La Rambla. Meanwhile, locals flock to the tiny bars on Carrer de Fraternitat for the real flavors.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still carrying a map, oblivious to the shortcut through Plaça de la Virreina that everyone else knows.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'sorry' when you bump into someone on the pavement. Here, it's a shrug and keep walking. Welcome to Dalston!
Had a bloke on the N136 last night arguing with the driver about the bus's 'soul'—only in Brixton, eh? Everyone else just nodding along.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full suit feeding pigeons while discussing crypto with a group of hipsters at Körnerpark.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a S-Bahn station with a DJ spinning records during peak hours. Just another Tuesday in Berlin, right?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they instinctively grab a burrito and a drink from El Farolito instead of heading to a corporate chain. It’s a habit.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Only in SF do you witness a woman in a full wedding dress trying to catch a Muni, while her friends lag behind with the cake.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the hidden plazas where locals sip vermut and laugh long into the night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll find them staring at Google Maps in the middle of La Rambla, completely oblivious to the crowd weaving around them.
